title: ischaemum dactyloideum
intro: Ischaemum dactyloideum, also known as the Blue Grana or Blue Grama, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infection, and promoting wound healing. Ischaemum dactyloideum can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its leaves and stems are often used in traditional medicine to create topical preparations and remedies.
